title: Tax Preparation Services

narrative.executive_view: Consumer tax preparation is a poor AI migration target despite high automatable labor. Simple returns face free guided software and AI-enabled DIY options, while only 40 deterministic firms reach the $1–10M EBITDA band. Storefront value rests more on trust, refund-related products and complex exceptions than form completion [S1][S2].
narrative.how_ai_changes_work: Interview, intake, document extraction, return preparation, status calls and seasonal scheduling are highly automatable. H&R Block nevertheless sells assisted preparation with guarantees and additional refund products [S2], showing that a human-service layer remains for some customers. The supplied 5412 role mix is CPA-dominated and does not represent seasonal preparers, so the apparent precision of V is explicitly limited.
narrative.value_capture: Flat per-return prices compete with free alternatives. IRS Free File has eight private-sector partners for 2026 and offers eligibility up to $89,000 AGI [S1], while Direct File itself is suspended for the 2026 season [S3]. Labor savings are therefore likely competed away in simple returns; refund transfers, advances and related services can retain value but are product/regulatory dependent [S2].
narrative.adoption_timing: The core workflow is already software-mediated and H&R Block markets AI assistance, so effective automation for simple-return production is estimated above 50%, making t50 exactly zero. This does not mean every franchise or independent has fully automated intake or that all customers will self-serve.
narrative.consolidation_economics: The deterministic band count is only 40 and comes from 100–499 employee firms, not typical single storefronts. H&R Block franchisees pay approximately 30% of gross tax-preparation and related-service revenue as royalty [S2], illustrating why franchise resale economics cannot be treated as independent-practice EBITDA. A local acquisition may not own the brand, client relationship or product economics.
narrative.terminal_demand: Terminal demand is melting for simple paid 1040 preparation: the government form, free filing options and increasingly capable software remove much of the reason to pay. Demand can remain for complex, immigrant/ITIN, dispute-resolution and refund-product niches, but these are narrower and not a license-protected broad service. This is separate from AI's labor-saving effect.
narrative.risks_and_uncertainty: Direct File was suspended rather than expanded, which weakens one disruption channel but does not remove Free File or commercial DIY pressure [S1][S3]. The code's seasonal workforce, owner SDE and franchise royalty loads make annual margin comparisons fragile. No reopened independent fee study or age census supports precision in pricing, ownership or succession inputs.

sources.title: IRS Free File 2026 announcement
sources.what_it_supports: 2026 free guided filing, eight partners and eligibility terms.

sources.title: H&R Block 2025 Form 10-K
sources.what_it_supports: Assisted preparation guarantees, franchise royalty rate and refund-product offerings.

sources.title: GAO decision on suspension of IRS Direct File
sources.what_it_supports: Direct File closure/suspension for the 2026 season and prior scope.

ai_replaceable_share.rationale: High automation estimate for interview-driven returns and intake; supplied CPA-heavy 5412 role weights are an imperfect constraint.
caveats: Actual 541213 workforce is seasonal preparers/intake, unlike 4-digit CPA-heavy role mix.

role_judgments.rationale: Review and complex exceptions remain human.
Simple form-driven preparation automates; due diligence remains.
Operations and product management retain human work.
Analysis assistance, but business judgment stays.
Seasonal intake/marketing tail is substantially automatable.

pi_dist.rationale: Free guided alternatives pressure simple-return prices; refund products and trust retain a partial share.
caveats: Free File eligibility and offerings vary; H&R Block is a major-chain proxy rather than all independents.

pi_moat.rationale: Brand, in-person assistance and refund products offer limited moat against free/DIY preparation.
caveats: Franchise and independent client-file ownership differs.

t50_years.rationale: Software-led/free filing and AI-assisted provider workflows imply over-50% effective automation for simple-return production.
caveats: Not a code-level adoption census; complex work is less automated.

current_adoption_pct.rationale: Estimated above 50% from widespread guided software/free filing and large-provider digital workflow, not direct survey observation.

historical_analogs.value: Online travel-style disintermediation: simple standardized transactions migrate to self-service while exceptions retain assistance.
historical_analogs.rationale: Free guided filing and assisted provider products show coexistence of self-service and support.
historical_analogs.plausible_range: Complexity and regulation can slow displacement.

selection.rationale: No reopened tax-preparer owner-age source; uncertain placeholder receives no source credit.

succession_shortage_documented.rationale: No reopened source documents a code-specific succession shortage.
caveats: Absence of evidence is not evidence of no issue.

active_consolidators.rationale: Major branded/franchise network activity supports a small consolidator count, limited to B.
caveats: Networks need not acquire independent 541213 firms.

buy_mult.rationale: Low multiple reflects terminal pressure, seasonality and royalty/product dependence.
caveats: No reopened code-specific sale comparables; SDE and EBITDA differ.

exit_mult.rationale: No multiple expansion assumed amid simple-return disruption.

pricing_structure.value: Flat per-return fees plus refund-related products; franchise units may pay royalties.
pricing_structure.rationale: IRS shows free guided alternatives; H&R Block documents refund products and franchise royalties.
pricing_structure.plausible_range: Independent, franchise and complex-return economics differ.
caveats: H&R Block does not represent every tax preparer.

cross_checks.uplift_vs_A_consistent: Simple-return work is already software-mediated, so high task automation and t50=0 are directionally consistent.

terminal_value.justification: Free guided filing and software erode simple paid return preparation; remaining assisted/refund-product niches are narrower [S1][S2].

cross_checks.no_proxy_confirmed: Brand/network evidence is confined to buyability/multiples and capture; it does not determine task replaceability.
cross_checks.pricing_model_deflation: Free alternatives and flat-fee competition are reflected only in pi_dist/pi_moat.

dataset_mismatch.description: Supplied 5412 role mix is CPA-dominated and not representative of seasonal consumer-tax staffing.
sensitivity: Actual preparer/intake weights could make V higher than the proxy result.

top_uncertain_inputs.score_impact: A is 10 at zero and 5 at two years.
C changes sharply with price deflation.

reviewer_note: All score-driving URLs reopened 2026-07-21; current Direct File status is documented as suspended, not assumed expanded.
